content.text: The article is already pasted in full, so I write the notes straight from it — no need to fetch or search for anything.

input.content:
# Log batching techniques

1. Shard-local ring buffer — each worker owns a fixed-size ring buffer, so there is no shared-queue contention.
2. Adaptive flush interval — the flush interval shrinks as the buffer fills and grows as it drains, cutting syscalls.
3. Backpressure watermark — the producer blocks at the high watermark instead of dropping lines.
